issues.title: Incorrect table names
issues.line: select ***gh from my_table m leeeft join my_table2 t where m.id = t.id and m.value > 100;
issues.problem: The table names 'my_table' and 'my_table2' do not match the schema provided.
issues.fix: Replace 'my_table' and 'my_table2' with the correct table names from the schema.

issues.title: Incorrect table aliases
issues.line: select ***gh from my_table m leeeft join my_table2 t where m.id = t.id and m.value > 100;
issues.problem: The table aliases 'm' and 't' are not meaningful. They should be replaced with meaningful aliases.
issues.fix: Replace 'm' and 't' with meaningful aliases such as 'ft' for 'fact_transactions' and 'dm' for 'dim_merchant'.

issues.title: Incorrect join condition
issues.line: select ***gh from my_table m leeeft join my_table2 t where m.id = t.id and m.value > 100;
issues.problem: The join condition 'm.id = t.id' is not valid for the provided schema.
issues.fix: Replace 'm.id = t.id' with a valid join condition such as 'ft.merchant_id = dm.merchant_id'.

issues.title: Incorrect selection
issues.line: select ***gh from my_table m leeeft join my_table2 t where m.id = t.id and m.value > 100;
issues.problem: The selection '***gh' is not valid.
issues.fix: Replace '***gh' with the correct columns to select, such as 'dm.merchant_name, ft.amount'.

issues.title: Poor readability
issues.line: select ***gh from my_table m leeeft join my_table2 t where m.id = t.id and m.value > 100;
issues.problem: The query is not formatted properly and lacks meaningful aliases and comments.
issues.fix: Format the query properly, use meaningful aliases, and add comments where necessary.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT dm.merchant_name, ft.amount FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions ft LEFT JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant dm ON ft.merchant_id = dm.merchant_id WHERE ft.amount > 100;
review.summary: The query has multiple critical issues related to correctness and readability. The corrected query properly joins the fact_transactions and dim_merchant tables and selects meaningful columns.

issues.title: Incorrect table names
issues.problem: The query uses incorrect table names 'users' and 'orders' instead of 'sigma_analytics.dim_customer' and 'sigma_analytics.fact_transactions'.
issues.fix: Replace 'users' with 'sigma_analytics.dim_customer' and 'orders' with 'sigma_analytics.fact_transactions'.

issues.title: Missing join condition
issues.problem: The query uses implicit join syntax which is less readable and can lead to errors.
issues.fix: Use explicit JOIN syntax.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN with a subquery to calculate the average amount per user.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, making it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of the query and each clause.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, t.transaction_id AS order_id
FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer c
JOIN sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.amount > (
    SELECT AVG(amount)
    FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions
    WHERE customer_id = c.customer_id
);
review.summary: The query had several critical correctness issues due to incorrect table names and missing join conditions. It also contained a correlated subquery that could be optimized and lacked comments for readability.

issues.title: Incorrect table reference
issues.problem: The query references a table 'users' that does not exist in the provided schema.
issues.fix: Replace 'users' with the correct table name, such as 'sigma_analytics.fact_transactions'.

issues.title: Incorrect WHERE clause
issues.problem: The 'WHERE' clause is incorrectly placed before the 'FROM' clause.
issues.fix: Move the 'WHERE' clause after the 'FROM' clause.

issues.title: Missing JOIN for merchant names
issues.problem: The query does not join the 'dim_merchant' table to show merchant names instead of IDs.
issues.fix: Add a JOIN clause to include 'sigma_analytics.dim_merchant'.

issues.title: Potentially inefficient SELECT *
issues.problem: Using SELECT * can lead to performance issues by fetching unnecessary columns.
issues.fix: Specify the required columns instead of using SELECT *.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining its purpose and logic.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose and logic of the query.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT ft.transaction_id, ft.amount, ft.status, ft.merchant_id, ft.customer_id, ft.transaction_date, ft.payment_method, dm.merchant_name FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions ft JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant dm ON ft.merchant_id = dm.merchant_id WHERE ft.status = 'COMPLETED';
review.summary: The query had critical issues with table references and syntax. It also lacked necessary joins and comments, and used SELECT * which could impact performance.

issues.title: Missing JOIN condition
issues.problem: The query pairs every customer with every order due to the absence of a JOIN condition.
issues.fix: FROM customers JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id

issues.title: Implicit join syntax
issues.problem: Using the old implicit join syntax can lead to confusion and errors.
issues.fix: FROM customers JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id

issues.title: Missing table aliases
issues.line: SELECT customers.name, orders.order_date FROM customers, orders
issues.problem: Table aliases are not used, making the query harder to read and maintain.
issues.fix: SELECT c.name, o.order_date FROM dim_customer c JOIN dim_orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, o.transaction_date FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer c JOIN sigma_analytics.fact_transactions o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue due to missing join condition, and readability issues due to implicit join syntax and missing table aliases.

issues.title: Incorrect table used
issues.problem: The query is selecting from 'students' table instead of the intended 'sigma_analytics.dim_merchant' or 'sigma_analytics.fact_transactions' tables.
issues.fix: SELECT merchant_name FROM sigma_analytics.dim_merchant LIMIT 5;

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining its purpose or logic.
issues.fix:
-- Select top 5 merchant names
SELECT merchant_name FROM sigma_analytics.dim_merchant LIMIT 5;

issues.title: Inconsistent naming
issues.problem: The alias 'name' is used instead of 'merchant_name' which is the actual column name in the schema.
issues.fix:
-- Select top 5 merchant names
SELECT merchant_name FROM sigma_analytics.dim_merchant LIMIT 5;

review.corrected_sql:
-- Select top 5 merchant names
SELECT merchant_name FROM sigma_analytics.dim_merchant LIMIT 5;
review.summary: The query was using an incorrect table and lacked comments and consistent naming. Fixed the table and added comments for readability.
